name: DOGE 4h Session Impulse Hold Expansion v1
edge_mechanism: a session-aligned directional impulse reclaims fast structure, holds that improvement into the next bar, and only then is continuation entered
thesis: DOGE 4h is the cycle primary lane and the active hypotheses explicitly call for testing session-window continuation. In a 90% chop / 10% transition tape, the right expression is not first-break chasing; it is session-led impulse plus post-impulse hold confirmation so the strategy only participates when the move shows evidence of escaping chop.
invalidation_condition: If session-led impulses do not occur often enough, or if post-impulse holds repeatedly fail before any modest continuation, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A session creates a directional impulse, price reclaims fast structure and keeps it on the following bar, then extends modestly as participation improves.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in chop-heavy markets, certain sessions can dominate follow-through. Requiring a hold after the session impulse should remove many false starts while leaving enough room for continuation when the regime briefly improves.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is the best fit because it is the primary lane, expressive enough for session-led moves, and exactly the kind of chop-heavy transition market where post-impulse hold matters more than first-break bravado.

indicator_role_map.EMA_20: fast structure reclaim and hold line

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The market may remain rotational despite the session impulse, causing continuation entries to come after a move that was always going to fade.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If it trades but follow-through is weak, tighten the hold requirement around EMA_20 before changing the impulse threshold.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A moderate number of entries, several small first-target captures, and only occasional larger continuation legs when state improvement is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: DOGE 4h shows enough session-impulse hold sequences to clear screen density, and one cross-lane preserves the same grammar with cleaner structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trades, or repeated hold confirmations that fail before the first target.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but materially denser than prior breakout rituals because the logic is impulse plus single-bar hold

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit primary lane for session-window continuation after hold confirmation.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE 4h produces enough impulse-hold-expansion sequences with positive post-cost first-target behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h remains too chop-bound and session-led holds collapse immediately.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with several first-target captures and a smaller number of clean continuation legs.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same session-impulse grammar survives on a cleaner major.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves the same hold-and-expand sequence with cleaner but smaller moves.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h is too flat for the mechanism.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, cleaner state-change continuation than DOGE.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same idea survives a more expressive lane with stronger move amplitude.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h keeps the impulse-hold-expansion sequence with enough structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h turns the setup into late-entry noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count than DOGE, larger continuation legs.

long: Enter only if next bar low stays >= EMA_20 - 0.10 * ATR_14 and close remains above EMA_20
short: Enter only if next bar high stays <= EMA_20 + 0.10 * ATR_14 and close remains below EMA_20
